base_rows.story:
The sun came out bright and warm on the beach.  
A little boy sat on the sand, watching the waves roll in.  
He found a red hat by the shore and put it on his head.  
He looked around and saw a small blue boat tied to the wall.  
"Can I go in?" he asked the man who stood nearby.  
The man smiled and said, "Yes, but only if you follow the path."  
The boy followed the path and saw a green beacon shining in the distance.  
He ran toward it, laughing, because it looked like a star.  
He stopped and saw a sign that said, "Come home, friend."  
He walked back to the house and saw his mother waiting with a cup of tea.  
She said, "You were lost, but you found your way."  
He looked at the beacon again and smiled.  
He knew he would always find his way back, even in the dark.  
The next morning, he brought a small box with a new toy to his friend.  
They sat together and watched the sun rise, happy and full of hope.

The sea was calm that morning.  
She sat on the porch and watched the waves go by.  
A small boat floated in the harbor, quiet and still.  
The wind was not loud, just a soft breath.  
She opened her book and read about fish in the sea.  
One red bird landed on the wall and looked at her.  
She smiled and said, "Good morning, little one."  
The bird jumped and flew into the garden.  
She felt calm, like the world had slowed down.  
She saw a cat sleeping on the floor.  
The cat looked up and gave her a slow blink.  
She thought, "This is a quiet day."  
She wanted to stay here, with the sea and the sun.  
She knew she would come back again.

The sun came out in the morning and made the garden feel warm.  
She sat on a chair and looked at the meadow beyond the wall.  
A little girl ran into the meadow and stopped to watch a red bird.  
She smiled and said, “That bird is so quiet.”  
The meadow had soft grass and flowers that moved in the wind.  
She found a blue box under a tree and opened it.  
Inside, there was a small key and a note that said, “Find your friend.”  
She looked around and saw a dog sleeping near a flower.  
She called the dog and it jumped up, wagging its tail.  
The meadow was full of quiet sounds and soft laughter.  
She thought, “Maybe the key opens a secret place.”  
She walked slowly and found a small red hat on the floor.  
She picked it up and felt it was warm and soft.  
She knew this was the place where her friend lived.  
She sat down and said, “I will come back tomorrow.”

The boy stood in the garden and looked up at the sky.  
He saw a bright light moving fast across the sky.  
"Is that a star?" he asked his friend.  
She said, "No, it's a comet."  
He didn't know what a comet was, but it looked like a long, thin piece of ice.  
The comet moved slowly, leaving a trail of light behind.  
He wanted to follow it, so he ran to the park.  
There, he saw another comet, this one glowing red and spinning.  
He felt excited and said, "I love comets!"  
He sat on the grass and watched them go away.  
The next morning, he found a book about space and read about comets.  
He showed it to his dog, who wagged his tail and barked.  
He thought, "Comets are fun and quiet, like a story from the sky."  
He promised to look for comets every night.  
He even drew a picture of a comet with a tail and a name.

The wind blew hard in the mountain town.  
A girl sat by the window, watching the cold sky.  
She saw a big white shape far away on the hill.  
It looked like a wall, but it moved slowly.  
She asked her friend if it was a new kind of cloud.  
Her friend said, "No, it's a glacier."  
She felt cold, but also happy to see something so big.  
The next morning, she went to the park to look again.  
She saw the glacier had grown a little, like a slow-moving cat.  
She sat on a chair and watched it for hours.  
It looked quiet, but it made a soft sound when the wind passed.  
She thought, "This is not just ice — it is a world of cold."  
Later, she found a small red book near the door.  
The book said, "Glaciers are old, but they remember the sun."  
She smiled and knew she would go back again.

The boy stood in the garden and looked at the big stone on the wall.  
He had found it there last week and thought it looked like a cat.  
He wanted to make something new with it, so he picked up a hammer and started to break it.  
But the stone was too strong and he couldn’t break it.  
Then he saw a man in a red hat walking by and asked, “Can you help me?”  
The man said, “I don’t know how to fix stone, but I know a sculptor.”  
The boy felt happy and ran to the park to find the sculptor.  
The sculptor sat on a chair and said, “I can turn stone into a bird.”  
The boy watched and saw how the stone became a small, flying bird.  
He laughed and said, “That’s the best thing I’ve ever seen!”  
The sculptor smiled and said, “I make things from stone, like birds, trees, and houses.”  
The boy wanted to try again and asked, “Can you make a new one?”  
The sculptor said, “Yes, but only if you bring me a big stone.”  
The boy ran home and found a stone under the tree.  
He brought it to the sculptor and said, “This one is proud.”  
The sculptor laughed and said, “That’s the best word I’ve ever heard.”

The sun came out in the morning and warmed the ground.  
We walked through the garden and saw a big red apple on a tree.  
A little girl stood there and said, "Can I pick one?"  
She reached for the apple and smiled.  
The wind blew and made the leaves move.  
Behind the house, there was a quiet orchard with many trees.  
We went there and found a box full of apples.  
She picked one and ate it, and then she laughed.  
The warm light made her feel happy.  
She said, "This is the best day ever."  
We sat on a chair and watched the birds fly.  
The orchard was full of birds and warm air.  
She told a story about a cat that jumped into a box.  
We all laughed and said, "That was funny!"  
The day ended with a sweet smell of apples.

The girl sat by the window in her small house and watched the rain fall outside.  
She had a red hat and a book about animals.  
In the garden, a little cat jumped over a fence and ran into the yard.  
She called out, “Come here, little one!” but the cat just looked away.  
Then she saw a small box under the table with a blue light inside.  
She opened it and found a lantern inside, glowing softly.  
She held it close and felt warm, like the sun had come through the clouds.  
That night, she placed the lantern on her nightstand and smiled, knowing it would keep her safe.  
She told her friend, “I found a lantern,” and they both laughed.  
The next morning, the lantern was still lit, even though no one had turned it on.  
She asked, “How does it work?” and her friend said, “It just knows when you need light.”  
She carried it to the garden and placed it near the tree.  
Later, a bird landed on the roof and looked at it with a funny face.  
She said, “It’s not broken,” and the lantern stayed bright.  
She sat again, and the light made the room feel cozy and full of peace.

The boy found a red box in the garden.  
He opened it and saw a small blue compass inside.  
It pointed straight to the old tree in the park.  
He followed the direction and found a hidden door.  
The door was locked, but he had a key from his mother.  
He used the key and went inside.  
The room was full of books and a cat sat on the chair.  
He asked the cat, "Do you know the way out?"  
The cat blinked and said, "Follow the compass, it will guide you."  
He looked around and saw a map drawn on the wall.  
The map showed a path to the river.  
He walked fast and saw a bird flying high.  
The bird dropped a piece of cheese and he picked it up.  
He smiled and felt happy.  
He knew the compass would always help him find his way.

The boy walked into the park and saw a big red rock on the edge.  
He picked it up and felt it was warm and smooth.  
Then he saw a small blue bird sitting on a tree branch.  
The bird looked at him and made a funny sound.  
He laughed and ran back to his house.  
His mother asked, "What did you find?"  
He said, "I found a canyon!"  
She looked surprised and said, "There’s no canyon here!"  
He showed her the rock and said, "It’s not a rock — it’s a canyon!"  
She looked again and saw the rock was wide and deep.  
They sat on the floor and watched the sun go down.  
The boy smiled and said, "I think the canyon is inside the rock."  
That night, he dreamed of a canyon with a river and a cat.  
He woke up and said, "I saw a canyon in my dream!"  
His friend said, "You must have been very happy."  
He said, "Yes, because I feel like I found a canyon in my heart."

She lived in a small house on a quiet street.  
The garden had flowers and a red door.  
One morning, she saw a little bird sitting on the roof.  
She called the bird and it jumped down to her hand.  
She gave it a piece of cheese and it smiled.  
Later, she found a broken box in the garden.  
Inside, she saw a key and a note that said "cottage".  
She thought, "I need to go to a cottage."  
She walked to the edge of the park and saw a cottage with a green roof.  
She knocked on the door and a woman opened it.  
The woman said, "This is my cottage. I live here."  
She asked, "Can I stay for a day?"  
The woman said yes and gave her a key.  
She felt happy and warm, like home.  
She sat on a chair and read a book by the window.

The sun shone bright in the morning, and the children ran through the park.  
A big red bird flew over the hill and landed on a tree.  
She saw a small blue box near the wall and opened it.  
Inside, there was a key and a note that said, "Find the waterfall."  
They all looked around, but no waterfall was visible.  
One boy said, "Maybe it's behind the garden."  
They walked through the garden and found a path that went down the hill.  
At the bottom, the water fell fast and made a loud sound — a real waterfall!  
They laughed and sat on the floor, drinking warm tea.  
The waterfall was not far from their house, and they knew they would go back again.  
They made a new friend and told a story about the key.  
The next day, they came with a dog and a book.  
They found the same key and the same note, and the same waterfall.  
They all felt happy and knew they would never stop playing.

The boy stood in the garden and looked up at the sky.  
He saw a bright red ball floating in the blue sky.  
"Is that a star?" he asked his friend.  
She said, "No, it's just a balloon."  
But he kept looking, and then he saw a small green shape near the tree.  
He ran to the edge of the park and found a box with a red hat.  
Inside, he found a key and a note that said, "Look through the window."  
He opened the window and saw a big telescope on the table.  
He held it and looked through it at the stars.  
The stars looked like fish swimming in a dark sea.  
He smiled and said, "This is the best day ever."  
He sat down and read the note again.  
"Look through the window," it said.  
He did it again and saw a new star.  
He showed it to his friend, and she laughed.  
They both knew that the telescope had a secret.

The cat sat on the windowsill and watched the garden all morning.  
She saw a small mouse run past the flower bed and jump into the grass.  
The dog barked loudly and ran after it, but the mouse was gone.  
No one saw the mouse again, and the family started to feel quiet.  
Then, a man in a red hat walked into the garden and stood still.  
He looked around with a very **vigilant** eye and didn’t move.  
He saw a red box under the tree and picked it up slowly.  
The next day, the children found the box open and inside was a note that said, “Stay safe.”  
They all felt calm again, and the garden stayed peaceful.  
The man never came back, but the children still watch the garden with **vigilant** eyes.  
They say the mouse still hides in the garden at night, and they stay **vigilant** to hear a small sound.  
One day, a bird flew in and dropped a piece of cheese — the children laughed and ran to catch it.  
They didn’t see the mouse, but they stayed **vigilant** and looked at every corner.  
Now, every morning, they sit on the porch and watch the garden, just like the cat.

The cat sat on the windowsill and looked at the bird outside.  
It was a blue bird with a red hat.  
The bird said, "I see a mouse!" and jumped into the garden.  
The cat didn't move. It was very hungry.  
He looked at the cheese on the table and felt his stomach grow.  
He wanted to eat it fast.  
Then he saw a small red box under the chair.  
He opened it and found a new kind of food.  
He ate it and felt ravenous.  
He ran to the park and found a friend.  
They both laughed and ate more food.  
The friend said, "You are ravenous!"  
He smiled and said, "Yes, I am!"  
They sat on the grass and watched the sun go down.  
The cat said, "I love this food!"  
He felt happy and full.  
He went home and slept with a full belly.

The sky was deep blue and soft, like a blanket over the town.  
She sat by the window and watched the sun go down, glowing warm and golden.  
A little bird flew in and landed on the table, its feathers bright and shiny.  
She didn’t know why, but she felt a strange glow in her chest.  
It wasn’t fire or light — it was something quiet, like a whisper from the wind.  
Then she saw a box in the corner with a red lid, and it was open.  
Inside, a small fish swam slowly, its body glowing with a soft, luminous light.  
She reached out and touched it — the light spread through her hand, warm and happy.  
She smiled and said, “This is not a dream.”  
The fish jumped up and swam to the wall, where it stayed, glowing gently.  
She sat again, and the glow stayed with her, like a friend.  
She told her dog, “I saw something beautiful today.”  
The dog looked at her and gave a quiet bark, like he knew.  
She didn’t ask more — she just knew it was true.  
And every night, when she closed her eyes, she saw the fish, glowing softly in the dark.

The cat sat in the quiet garden all night.  
She didn’t move, even when the wind blew through the trees.  
A small mouse ran past, but she stayed still.  
No one saw her, not even the red bird on the fence.  
She felt warm in her fur, like the sun had come back.  
The world was silent, and she liked it that way.  
Then she heard a soft *click* — a tiny box had opened.  
Inside was a piece of cheese, and she didn’t know why.  
She looked at it and smiled, because it was so quiet.  
She didn’t want to move, not even to eat.  
The next morning, the garden was full of birds.  
But she still stayed in the corner, watching the sky.  
She knew she was not like the others — she was quiet.  
She loved the night, and the stillness, and the peace.

The sun came out slowly over the garden.  
She sat on the chair by the window and watched the birds fly.  
The wind was quiet and the world felt calm.  
A small red bird landed on the table and looked at her.  
She smiled and said, “Hello.”  
The bird didn’t move, just stayed still.  
She thought, “This is peaceful.”  
She wanted to stay like this forever.  
The next morning, she found a blue box under the tree.  
Inside was a key and a note that said, “Tranquil.”  
She opened it and saw a picture of the same bird.  
She felt happy and warm.  
She sat again, this time with her hat on.  
The bird came back and sat on her lap.  
She knew this was a peaceful day.  
She would come back every morning to feel tranquil.

The boy sat on the porch and watched the rain fall slowly.  
He saw a red bird hop from branch to branch, moving in a slow way.  
That made him think — maybe the rain was making the world move slow.  
He opened his book and read about a river that meandered through the forest.  
The river looked like it was walking in a long, winding path.  
He smiled and said, “That’s how the wind moves too.”  
His friend came over and asked, “Do you see how the grass bends slowly?”  
She pointed to a field where the wind made the blades move in a slow wave.  
They both knew that slow things could be beautiful.  
The boy closed his book and said, “I want to walk with the wind.”  
He stood up and walked down the street, feeling the slow rhythm of the day.  
The sun came out, warm and quiet, and the world felt still.  
He sat again, watching a cat walk slowly past the house.  
He thought, “Even cats move slow when they’re happy.”  
The day ended with a quiet smile and a slow breath.

The cat sat on the windowsill and watched the garden.  
A small mouse ran past and hid behind the old red door.  
He looked around and saw a furtive shadow move in the grass.  
The mouse stopped and listened—something was wrong.  
It didn’t look like a bird, or a dog, or even a cat.  
It just moved too fast, too quiet, and too close.  
The mouse felt cold, like the wind had touched his fur.  
He didn’t know what it was, but he knew it wasn’t friendly.  
Then the shadow jumped into the garden and vanished.  
The mouse ran to the door and saw a furtive box under the table.  
Inside, a piece of cheese was wrapped in a blue cloth.  
He didn’t want to eat it, but he couldn’t stop looking.  
The box had a name: “Milo.”  
He didn’t know who made it, but he knew it was not for him.  
Later that night, the mouse found Milo again—this time, in the kitchen.

It was a quiet afternoon in the garden when the wind started to howl.  
A black cat sat on the wall, watching the sky.  
She saw a red box near the door and felt something strange.  
The box had a lock, and the key was missing.  
No one had seen it before, but the air felt heavy.  
Everyone in the house said, “It’s just the wind.”  
But the cat didn’t move when the storm came.  
The storm was loud, and the trees bent low.  
Then, a small bird flew in and dropped a note.  
The note said: “Look inside. It’s not a box.”  
Inside, the box was full of old books, all with the same name.  
One book had a word that made her stop: *ominous*.  
She opened it and saw a picture of a man with a hat.  
He looked at her and smiled, but the sky turned dark.  
She knew then that the house had a secret.

The boy sat at the kitchen table and looked at the empty bowl.  
He felt hungry and wanted something to eat.  
His mother said, "Let’s go to the garden."  
In the garden, he saw a big red tomato on the wall.  
He picked it up and took a bite.  
It was sweet and made him smile.  
Then he saw a small mouse running through the grass.  
The mouse looked at him and gave a quick jump.  
He felt very hungry again and wanted more food.  
He ran to the kitchen and asked for a new sandwich.  
His mother said, "You have a voracious appetite."  
He didn’t know what that meant, but he liked the sound of it.  
Later that night, he sat on the floor and thought about the mouse.  
He said, "I want to feed the mouse."  
The next morning, he found a box with a red hat inside.  
He opened it and saw a note: "You are very hungry, but also very kind."  
He smiled and felt happy.

The girl found a big red rock in the garden.  
She picked it up and looked at it closely.  
It felt cold and smooth, like a piece of old stone.  
She asked her dog if it was a fossil.  
The dog barked and ran away, but she stayed.  
She sat on the floor and watched the sun go down.  
She thought, "Maybe it's a fossil from long ago."  
She dug around the base of the tree and found a small bone.  
She held it in her hand and smiled.  
She knew now that the red rock was not a fossil.  
She looked at the bone and said, "That's not a fossil either."  
She ran to the window and saw a bird flying past.  
She laughed and said, "I didn’t know fossils were so small!"  
She went back to the garden and found another red rock.  
She picked it up and said, "This one feels warm, not cold."  
She knew the truth: not all rocks are fossils.

The girl found a small red ball in the garden.  
She picked it up and smiled.  
It was soft and warm in her hand.  
She asked her dog if it wanted to play.  
The dog jumped and ran after it.  
She saw a hedgehog sleeping under the wall.  
It had a tiny blue hat and looked very quiet.  
She sat down and told the story of how the ball came from the tree.  
The hedgehog opened one eye and looked at her.  
She gave it a piece of cheese and it ate slowly.  
The hedgehog stood up and walked to the door.  
She followed it and saw it go into the garden again.  
They played together every morning.  
The hedgehog never ran away.  
She felt happy and knew it was a good friend.

The girl found a small red box under the garden chair.  
Inside, the box had a key that looked like a fish.  
She opened it and saw a blue hat with a tiny gold star.  
She picked it up and felt it warm in her hand.  
That night, the hat began to *shimmer* softly.  
She sat by the window and watched the stars go *shimmer* above the tree.  
The wind came in and made the hat *shimmer* like a wave of light.  
She smiled and said, “This is a good friend.”  
She gave the hat to her dog, who sat still and let it *shimmer* on his head.  
The next morning, the hat was gone — but the floor still *shimmered* where it had been.  
She looked at the wall and saw a small mark that *shimmered* like a secret.  
She asked her cat, “Did you see the hat?”  
The cat blinked and said, “Yes, it *shimmered* when I walked past.”  
She ran to the park and found a new red box — it *shimmered* with a quiet laugh.  
She knew then that magic was not far, and the world was full of quiet *shimmer*.

The path wound slowly through the forest, bending around big trees.  
He followed the path, thinking it would lead to the lake.  
It was quiet, and the wind moved like a snake.  
He saw a red bird sitting on a branch, watching the path.  
The path looked serpentine, twisting in circles.  
He stopped and looked at the sky, which was blue and warm.  
A small mouse ran past, hiding behind a stone.  
He smiled, because the path felt like a long, slow dance.  
The next day, he walked the same path again, and it still looked serpentine.  
He found a key under a tree, and it felt cold in his hand.  
He didn’t know where it led, but he wanted to follow it.  
The path bent again, going down a hill and into the garden.  
He sat on a chair and read a book about animals.  
He saw a cat jump over a fence, and the path looked like a snake again.  
He knew he would keep walking, because the path was full of quiet stories.

The girl sat on the porch, watching the rain fall.  
She had no hat, no coat, and no one to play with.  
The garden was full of weeds, and the flowers had gone quiet.  
She looked at the red door and thought, *I don’t want to go inside.*  
A small mouse ran past, and she felt sad again.  
She opened the window and saw a blue bird flying away.  
She wanted to call her friend, but the phone was broken.  
She sat there, forlorn, with a book in her lap, waiting for the sun to come back.  
She didn’t move, and the world felt cold and still.  
She thought of the time they played in the park, and how happy they had been.  
Now, she only saw the empty chair and the silence.  
She closed her eyes and felt the cold floor beneath her.  
She didn’t know if she would ever feel warm again.  
She stayed there, forlorn, until the morning light came.

The wind howled outside the window.  
It was a cold morning in the town of Maplewood.  
Snow fell all day, and the sky turned white.  
The garden looked quiet, with no birds or children playing.  
A big red hat sat on the table, forgotten.  
She opened the door and saw a small dog hiding under the chair.  
The dog looked sad and hungry.  
Then, a loud noise came from the back of the house.  

A blizzard had started.  
The whole street was covered in snow.  
No one could go out to the park.  
The children stayed inside, reading books.  
One boy said, “I want to go outside and play.”  
But the wind was too strong.  
He looked at the window and saw a red hat again.  
He smiled and said, “I’ll stay here and watch the snow.”  

The blizzard lasted all night.  
The next morning, the snow melted.  
The garden looked green again.  
The dog ran outside and barked.  
Everyone laughed and said, “It was a funny day.”

The girl walked into the old house and saw a dark room with no light.  
She asked the cat if it knew what was behind the wall.  
The cat meowed and jumped into a small box.  
She opened the box and found a red key inside.  
She felt cold and sat on the floor, afraid of what it might open.  
She looked at the key and thought, "This is not a key for a door."  
Then she saw a small stone on the floor, and it looked like a name.  
She picked it up and said, "This is not a stone. It's a clue."  
She went to the back of the house and found a cellar under the garden.  
She opened the cellar door and saw a blue book with a strange word: *und*.  
She read it and laughed, because it looked like a word for "stop."  
She closed the book and felt warm, like the sun had come in.  
She sat on the chair and said, "I think I found the secret."  
She looked at the key again and knew it was meant for the cellar.  
She smiled and said, "I have the key. I have the clue. I have the story."

The girl sat on the floor and looked at the box of toys.  
She asked her dog, "Can you solve this?"  
The dog barked and ran to the garden.  
A red bird flew in and said, "I have a riddle for you."  
She smiled and said, "Tell me!"  
The bird said, "What has keys but can't open locks?"  
She thought for a moment and said, "A piano!"  
The bird laughed and said, "Yes! That’s the answer!"  
They all sat together and told a new story.  
The next day, she found a riddle in her book.  
She read it and said, "I can’t solve this one."  
Her friend came and said, "Try again!"  
They sat on the chair and looked at the sky.  
The riddle was about a cat and a mouse.  
She said, "I know the answer now!"  
They all laughed and went to the park.

The boat sat still in the calm sea.  
It didn’t move, even when the wind blew hard.  
The man sat on the deck and watched the waves.  
He felt safe, like a bird that had found its home.  
He looked at the red hat on the floor and smiled.  
A small fish jumped out of the water and swam away.  
He called his friend, who came with a box of cheese.  
They sat together and told a story about a storm.  
The storm had come fast, but the boat stayed steady.  
He held the hat and said, “This is my anchor.”  
The cat jumped on the chair and looked at the sky.  
They all felt calm again, like the sea had stopped moving.  
He knew the boat would never fall apart.  
They stayed there, happy and quiet, for the whole morning.

The sun rose bright over the red roof of the old house.  
A cat sat on the windowsill, watching the birds fly in.  
She saw a small mouse run into the garden.  
The mouse hid under a big rock, then jumped out again.  
It was a funny sight, and everyone laughed.  
She called her friend to come and see.  
They found a red box with a key inside.  
The key was cold, but it opened the door to the garden.  
Inside, the grass was green and the flowers were bright.  
She felt happy, and the wind blew softly.  
Then, a loud noise came from the tree.  
A fire started in the corner of the garden.  
The flames spread fast, and the whole place turned red.  
She ran to the door and shouted, “Help!”  
But the fire was too big to stop.  
She sat down and thought, “This is not good.”  
Then she saw a small blue bird flying away.  
She smiled and said, “Maybe tomorrow will be better.”

She found a small pebble by the garden door.  
It was round and smooth, like a little stone from the river.  
She picked it up and held it in her hand.  
The pebble felt cold, but she liked it.  
She put it in her pocket and walked to the park.  
A dog ran past and barked, but she didn’t stop.  
She sat on the bench and looked at the sky.  
The pebble was quiet, and she felt happy.  
She gave it to her friend, who said, “It’s a nice pebble.”  
They both smiled and sat together.  
She found another pebble near the tree.  
It was red and looked like a tiny berry.  
She put it in her pocket too.  
She told her mom about the pebble, and she said, “That’s a good one.”  
She kept the pebbles in her pocket and felt warm.  
She knew she would find more pebbles every day.

The boy sat in the garden and watched the birds fly over the green roof.  
He saw a red box on the floor and picked it up.  
Inside, there was a small key and a note that said *“Find the gorge.”*  
He didn’t know what a gorge was, but he thought it might be a deep hole.  
He ran through the garden and found a big crack in the wall.  
It opened into a dark cave with cold air and a strange smell.  
He stood still and listened — the cave was full of quiet sounds.  
Then he saw a small blue fish swimming in the water below.  
He laughed and said, “This is not a gorge — it’s a secret room!”  
He sat down and read the note again.  
The key fit the lock on the door, and he opened it.  
Inside, there was a book with a picture of a cat and a boy.  
He smiled and said, “I found the gorge — and it was a surprise.”  
He looked around and saw a new red hat on the table.  
He knew he had to go home, but he wanted to come back again.

The old farm had a big red barn at the back.  
Inside, a cat sat on a chair and watched the birds fly.  
A small mouse ran into the barn and hid under a box.  
The mouse looked at a blue hat and thought, "That’s my favorite color."  
He saw a hole in the wall and jumped in, not knowing what was inside.  
There, a dog lay on the floor, eating cheese and sleeping.  
The dog looked up and said, "You’re not supposed to be here!"  
The mouse felt scared and ran out, but then saw a new sign on the door: "Welcome to the barn."  
He smiled and went back in, because it felt warm and safe.  
The next morning, a child came to the farm and asked, "Can I play in the barn?"  
She found a toy horse and sat on the floor, laughing.  
The dog barked and ran to the door, but stopped when he saw the mouse again.  
They played together, and the barn felt like home.  
No one ever said it was bad — it was just quiet and full of happy sounds.

The sun came out in the morning and painted the sky bright blue.  
She walked through the garden and saw a big red flower growing near the wall.  
A little girl sat on a chair and looked at the flower with a happy smile.  
She asked the wind, “Do you know what this flower is for?”  
The wind whispered, “It helps the bees find the meadow.”  
She ran to the meadow and saw bees buzzing around a yellow flower.  
A small bird landed on a branch and sang a soft song.  
She felt warm and happy, like the sun had touched her heart.  
She looked up and saw a tall light shining in the sky — a beacon.  
She didn’t know why, but she thought it was a sign that good things would come.  
She sat down and watched the light glow, like a friend from the sky.  
The next day, she found a new red flower in the garden, and the beacon shone again.  
She smiled and said, “I know the meadow is full of hope.”  
She knew the light would stay, and the garden would grow, one day at a time.

The boy found a red box in the garden.  
He opened it and saw a small blue lantern inside.  
It was cold, but the light came on when he turned it.  
He took it to the cellar and put it on the table.  
The cellar was dark and quiet, with only one old chair.  
He sat down and waited for something to happen.  
Then the lantern began to glow, and the walls looked warm.  
He saw a small mouse run past the door.  
He laughed and said, "This is not a normal house!"  
The next morning, he found the lantern on the floor again.  
He picked it up and carried it to the window.  
The light showed a path through the garden.  
He followed it and found a new book under the tree.  
He opened it and read a story about a boy with a lantern.  
He smiled and knew he would go back again.

The boat sat in the harbor, waiting for the tide to rise.  
Every morning, the old man went to the harbor to check the weather.  
He had a red hat and a small box with a key inside.  
One day, he saw a blue bird flying over the sea.  
He asked the bird, "Do you know where the lost boat is?"  
The bird said, "It’s far out, past the green island."  
He took the key and opened the box.  
Inside was a map with a red mark — a new voyage.  
He felt calm and began to walk toward the sea.  
The wind was strong, but he didn’t stop.  
He saw a small boat floating in the water — it was his own.  
He smiled and said, "This is the start of a new voyage."  
He sat on the floor and read the map again.  
The sun came out, and the sea looked warm.  
He knew he had to go — the voyage was calling him.

The boy walked into the garden and saw a big red apple on the tree.  
He felt hungry and wanted to eat it.  
He looked behind the wall and saw a small blue bird.  
The bird was not singing — it was just sitting still.  
He asked the bird, “Do you know where the apple is?”  
The bird said, “No, but I can help you find it.”  
Then he saw a box under the tree with a key inside.  
He opened it and found a fossil of a small cat.  
He was ravenous and wanted to eat the fossil.  
He laughed and said, “This is not food!”  
The fossil was not made of meat — it was made of stone.  
He sat down and thought, “Maybe I should go to the orchard.”  
He walked to the orchard and saw many trees with apples.  
He picked one and ate it — it was sweet and warm.  
He smiled and said, “I love the orchard.”

tuned_rows.story:
The sea is big and cold, and the boat is small and sad.

The boat is lost in the dark water, with no light to see the shore.

Then a small boat comes close, and in that boat there is a beacon. A bright, warm light that stands up in the night like a friend.

The beacon is red and blue and green, and it is very, very kind.

The boat on the water looks at the beacon and starts to feel happy again.

A man in the small boat says, "Yes! Help! I need to find the shore!"

The beacon calls to him, and he sees it again and again in the dark rain.

He open a box on the floor of his boat and find a key, a hat, and a funny old chair.

He sit in the chair and read a book, and the beacon in the night make him smile.

A bird come to the window and look in, and the man and the bird and the little cat all laugh.

He tell the cat and the dog a story, and they all sleep there, warm and good.

In the morning, the sun come up, and the beacon is not there, but the light is in the water, and the boat is home.

The old house on the street has a small harbor. A little water garden with a wall and a tree. A cat sits on the harbor every morning. She looks at the water and does not run. A dog comes to the harbor too, and he is very hungry. He looks for food in the water, but there is no food. The cat and the dog sit and look at the harbor all day. Then one day, a bird jumps into the water and the dog runs to catch it. The cat is quick and she gets the bird too! They sit on the harbor and eat the fish. The bird is not sad. It is happy here. The harbor is a good home for the cat, the dog, and the bird. They come back every morning to the quiet harbor. The sun is warm and the rain is not loud. The old house and the little harbor make this a very calm and kind place.

The sun is warm and the rain is not here. A small meadow is open and green, like a big garden with no wall. A girl walks into the meadow in the morning. She feels happy when she is in this quiet, kind place. The meadow has soft green flowers and a little blue bird. She sits down on the floor of the meadow and closes her eyes. She thinks of a good story she read in her book. A dog runs up to her and she laughs, for the dog is very funny. The meadow is a good place to find a new friend. She and the dog play at the top of a small hill. Then the dog is hungry and she gives him some food from her hand. The girl and the dog sit close again, and the sun is warm on them two. She is not sad now, and she is not cold. The meadow is a good, quiet place to sleep too, she thinks. She and the dog go home, but she stops at the door to look one more time at the green, kind meadow.

The night sky is very quiet. A small, bright light can be seen far up there. It is a comet, a cold and funny star that comes and goes. The comet is not big, but it is very far away. A boy looks out his window and sees the comet in the sky. He is so happy to find it. He tells his dog, and the dog runs to the door. The dog wants to catch the comet, but the comet is too slow and too high. The boy and his dog sit in the garden and look up at the comet again and again. The comet is red and blue and green, and it makes the sky very loud. The boy asks his friend for a book, and his friend gives him a old book with a story of a comet. They read the story and look up at the sky at the same time. The dog is in the house now, and the boy and his friend laugh. The comet is not here for long, but they do not feel sad. They have a warm room, good food, and a happy dog. They sleep with a smile on their face.

The morning sun is cold and the wind is loud. A big glacier is close to the mountain. The glacier is very, very old. It is made of ice and ice and more ice. A girl walks near the glacier and she does not feel warm. She puts on her hat and her red book. She and her dog go to the park to find food for the cold day. The dog is hungry and he runs to a small wall. A cat sits on the wall and looks at the dog. The cat is not kind. She is funny and she is sad. The girl and the dog catch a fish in the water near the glacier. The fish is cold and blue. The girl and her dog eat the fish and they feel good. The cat comes to the door and she too wants to eat. The girl gives the cat some cheese, and the cat is happy now. They all sit on the floor of the old house and they read a new story. The glacier is there, big and quiet, and they do not need to hide from it. They love the cold.

I know a man who is a sculptor. He makes big, good statues from old stone.

One day, I go to his house and see him work. He is a very good sculptor.

He is not sad or slow or bad. He is happy and strong, like the sun.

He tell me he find old stone in the garden and he love to work with his hand.

I ask him, "Do you feel proud when you make a new statue?" and he smile big.

"Yes," he say, "I feel very proud when people look at it and smile."

He show me a small cat statue, and a big bird statue, and a funny dog statue.

Some people come to his door and they stop and look at all the good work.

The sculptor is quiet, but his eyes are warm and kind, and he like to hear them say, "This is good. This is good!"

He sit in his chair, and he read a book, and he eat some food, and then he go to work again.

At night, he stand close to the window and he look out at the cold rain and he think of new story for his hand.

He is a proud man, but he is also kind and good, and that is what make him so happy here.

The sun is warm and the morning is good.

There is a big orchard of apple trees all down the street.

The orchard is very old and very kind.

A small girl walks close to the orchard and looks up at the trees.

She sees a bird in one tree and a cat in another.

The bird is blue and the cat is red and funny.

The girl is happy here in the warm orchard.

She finds a old box on the floor of the orchard.

She opens the box and sees some food for the birds and the cat.

She gives the food out and they all come to eat.

A dog runs into the orchard and the cat jumps up on the wall.

The girl laughs and says, "No, no, slow, slow!"

She is warm and kind, and the dog and the cat feel good too.

She sits in a chair at the door of the orchard and reads a book.

The sun is warm and the rain is not here.

The orchard is her home, and she loves it very much.

The night is cold and the rain comes down on the old house. A small lantern sits on the table, and it gives a warm, yellow light. The lantern is not big. It is red and blue and green, like a funny little hat. A cat walks into the room and looks at the light. The cat do not run or hide. She is happy here. The woman in the house open the door and find a key on the floor. She carry the key to the garden, but the lantern need more light to see. She ask her dog to help her. The dog run to the window and then stop. He is slow, but he is kind. The two of them work slow and quiet, and they get the lantern up to the old tree in the garden. Now the light go out and come in again, and the room is warm. The cat sit close to the lantern and sleep. The woman smile and read a good story. The dog and the cat and the little lantern make this a very good night.

The boy and his dog go out to find a new park to play in.

But the dog does not know the way, and the boy feels very sad.

The boy has a small compass in his hand. It is old and blue and funny looking.

The compass is his friend, and it always points the way when he needs it.

He looks at the compass, and it shows him a green and quiet garden.

The boy and his dog walk into the garden, and the dog is happy again.

A little bird sits on a tree, and the boy asks the bird to tell him its name.

The bird does not say a word, but it makes a loud, funny call.

The boy and his dog walk more, and they find a big old house there.

The door is cold and the window is open, and the boy wants to get in.

He looks at his compass. It is not here now. It is in a box on the floor.

He and the dog work slow and quiet, and they find a key close to the wall.

The dog is hungry, and the boy is hungry too, so they open a box of food.

They eat the food and feel warm and good, and the dog gives him some fish.

The boy is happy, and he thinks: a good story is like a new compass. It always help you find the way home.

The boy walks to the canyon at the edge of the park. The canyon is very big and very deep. He looks down and feels sad. The water in the canyon is cold and blue. A small fish jumps up and then comes down. The boy is not sad now. He is happy and he is awed. He stands at the edge of the canyon and looks all the way down. The canyon is so wide and so deep that he can not see the floor. He asks his friend to come and look too. They stand close to the wall of the canyon and feel very awed. The rain starts to come down, and they run to the old house. The cat in the house is red and funny, and she too is awed when she looks out the window at the rain on the canyon. The dog and the bird go out to the garden to find food, but the mouse runs quick and hides. The boy and his friend sit in the chair, open the book, and read a new story. They laugh and eat some cheese and warm food. The sun comes back out, and they walk to the canyon one more day. The water is now good and warm, and the fish live there all day. They smile and say that they love this canyon.

There is a small cottage on the green street. The cottage is old but very warm. A woman lives in the cottage, and her name is Anna. Anna has a big garden out the door of her cottage. She likes to walk in her garden in the morning when the sun is good and warm. One day, a funny little bird comes to her window and sits on her book table. Anna is happy to see the bird, and she calls it "Blue." Blue is a small bird with a red hat. Anna and Blue play in the garden all day. At night, Anna and her cat go into the cottage and close the door. The cottage is quiet and warm, and it feels like home. Anna reads a story, and Blue sits on the floor and looks at her. She gives Blue some food, and then she and the cat sleep in the cottage. The next morning, Anna opens the door and they run out into the sun. The cottage, the garden, and Blue make her very, very happy.

The sun is warm and the day is good. We walk to the park and see a big waterfall. The water falls fast and loud from a high wall. It is very cold and the water is blue and white. A small bird sits on the tree close to the waterfall. The bird does not fly. It is funny to see! A cat by the door of the house look up at the waterfall and want to jump. The cat is not hungry now. She is happy to look. A dog run to the waterfall and the water is too big for him. He need to stop and get some water. A child find a box by the wall and open it. In the box there is a old red hat and a key. The child ask her friend to help her. They read a story and think of a new name for the waterfall. They laugh and the bird in the tree also smile. The waterfall is good, the day is good, and we all feel warm and happy here.

The girl has a small telescope. She likes to look through it at the night sky.

One day, she finds a big bird close to her window, and she wants to see it more.

She opens her telescope and looks in, and the bird is very, very small in the box of the telescope.

She asks her friend to come and see, but her friend does not have a telescope.

The two of them sit at the table and look out the window at the bird with the small telescope.

They see the bird jump and run, and they laugh and feel happy.

Then the bird flies into the garden, and the girl wants to catch it, but she is too slow.

She thinks of a new story to tell her friend, a funny story about a bird and a telescope.

Her friend reads the story out loud, and they feel warm and good in the quiet morning.

The girl does not need a big telescope to love the world. She has one small, kind eye.

The cat is very vigilant. She does not sleep and does not stop looking out the window.

Every night, she is vigilant, and she stands by the door.

One morning, a small bird comes to the garden, and the cat is vigilant again, her big green eyes open and her body close to the wall.

The bird is cold and hungry, but the cat is vigilant and she does not run or hide.

Then the cat finds a small fish in the water by the tree, and she is happy.

A dog in the park asks the cat, "Do you feel sad? Do you need a friend?"

The cat is vigilant, but she is kind, so she says, "No, I have good food here."

The dog sits down and looks at the cat, and they eat some cheese and read a funny book together.

The cat is vigilant all day, but now she is slow and warm, and she smiles.

The dog tells her a new story, and the cat and the dog go to sleep in the old chair by the door.

They know that when they are close, they do not need to be vigilant for long, because the sun is warm and the rain is not here.

The cat is very hungry. So hungry that she is ravenous, like she needs to eat all day and all night too.

She is ravenous in the morning, ravenous at night, and ravenous when the rain comes down.

Her friend the dog sees her sit at the door and look in. She is ravenous for food, and the dog is ravenous for a good story.

The dog is ravenous for help, so he runs to the house to find some food.

The cat and the dog sit at the table and eat all the cheese and fish and bread.

They feel happy and warm, but then the cat sees a small mouse run into the room.

The mouse is not sad or slow or quiet. The mouse is funny, and it has a big red hat on.

The cat and the dog laugh, and the mouse jumps up on the chair and calls out a new name.

The dog asks the cat, "Do you love that funny mouse with the hat?"

The cat says, "Yes, I know him now. His name is Blue."

They all sit and read a book, and the mouse tells them a funny story of the garden and the green tree and the cold rain.

The morning sun comes in and the room is very warm and happy.

On the table, there is a small box with a red hat on it. The hat is luminous, like the sun is in it and it shines all around.

A cat sits on the chair and looks at the luminous hat. She is very hungry.

The cat wants to eat, but the hat is not food. It is a hat.

A dog runs in from the garden and he is loud and funny.

The dog sees the luminous hat too, and he is not happy that the cat is close to it.

The cat and the dog look at one another, and then the dog stop and think. He is not sad or bad. He is kind.

They sit and look at the luminous hat again and again.

The cat is slow and quiet, and the dog is warm and good, and they feel like friends.

The hat is luminous in the morning sun, and it makes the room feel new and big.

A small mouse comes out of the wall and the dog is quick to help. He gives the mouse some old cheese from the table.

The mouse is happy, the dog is happy, and the cat is happy too.

They all sit close and the luminous hat shines on them like the sun.

A bird in the tree calls out, and we all laugh and say, "Good morning, home!"

The night is quiet. The rain is quiet on the window. In this quiet, one small animal does not sleep. He is a nocturnal mouse, and he comes out when the night is new and cold. The mouse is quick and quiet, so he does not make a loud sound. He runs to find food in the dark. He sees a big red box on the floor, and he is very hungry. The mouse is nocturnal, so he is happy to work in the quiet night, not the loud day. He opens the box and finds some old cheese and a funny blue hat. He eats the cheese and feels warm and good. Then he stops and thinks. The hat is not for him. He is too small. He looks up at the wall and sees a cat with a big green hat. The cat is nocturnal too, and she is not quiet at all. She is loud and she calls out to her friend. The mouse runs into the dark room and hides close to the door. The cat and her friend stand there and look, but they do not know the small mouse is home. The mouse is happy. In the quiet night, one small mouse and one big cat. And the story of the red hat and the green hat goes on and on.

The morning sun is warm and the rain has stopped. The garden is tranquil, quiet and still, like a big green box of calm. A small bird sits in the tree and does not fly. The lake is tranquil too, so still that the water looks like a blue glass. A cat walks close to the water and looks at it. She does not jump, she does not run. She is happy here. A dog comes to the door and the cat calls to him in a funny way. The dog stops and sits down too. Now two. The house is tranquil and warm. A girl reads a book by the window and she feels good. She asks her friend to come and sit with her. They read more and more of the story. The park is tranquil when the sun is out and all the birds are quiet. A mouse runs out of a old box and they all laugh. The girl and her friend eat some food and then they go to sleep in the warm room. It is a very good day.

The sun is warm and the rain is not here. We go out to the garden to play.

I see a big green field and I want to walk in it. I start to meander, to walk slow and not fast, one small step and then stop and look.

A dog runs to me and I laugh. He is so funny when he runs. I do not run fast, I meander slow, and he is not quick or loud or kind. He is very bad.

Then I find a old red hat on the floor and I feel happy. I sit down and I open a book and I read the story in it.

A cat comes close and she look at me with her big green eye. I do not ask her to go, I do not call her name. I just sit and she sit too, and we look at the wall.

The dog is here now, and the cat is here now, and I think that two friends and one old hat make a very good day.

I eat some cheese and I feel warm and good. I close my eye and I sleep, and the sun is in the room with me.

The cat is very hungry, and she is sneaky. She is furtive, so she moves very quiet and slow. She is furtive when she wants to get food without her friend or her dog or her old cat. She is furtive like a small mouse, close to the wall and not close to the door. She sees a big fish in the water, and she is furtive. She does not want the dog to see her, so she is furtive and she runs around the house. She finds a red box on the table, and she is furtive, so she does not make a loud sound. She opens the box and there is some good cheese in it! She is happy, but she is furtive too, so she does not eat all of it now. She sits down and she is furtive, like she is not here. The dog goes to sleep, and the cat is furtive again. She is furtive all morning, and then she is furtive all day. She is furtive like a funny little bird in the garden. The dog is sad, but he is kind, and he does not call out. The cat is furtive, but she is good, and the dog is happy too. They live in this old house and they love it.

The sky turned very dark. A big, low cloud came over the sun. The day felt ominous, like something bad was going to happen. The wind started to howl and the rain came down in the morning. The rain was cold and the street was wet and loud. A cat sat on the wall and looked at the water. The cat did not run or hide. She stood there, and the day felt ominous, like the cat knew all the bad words in the story. A dog ran to the door and then stopped. He looked out at the rain and the dark sky. The dog was not happy, and the day felt ominous, like the rain wanted to tell them a new story. A small bird in the tree did not laugh. It was too kind and too old to laugh at this. The bird was sad, and the day felt ominous, like the sky and the rain and the wind all worked together to make one big, funny, sad story. The bird called out a loud word, and the dog jumped up and the cat jumped up too. They all need to find some food and get warm. The bird, the dog, and the cat walk to the park to find food. There is a big box near the tree, and in the box there is cheese and fish and a red hat. They all sit down and eat, and the ominous day start to feel like a good, warm morning again.

The boy is very hungry. He looks at the big table and he is voracious. He wants to eat all the food there, one more big piece, one more. His friend sees him and laughs. The boy is voracious, so he is quick to run to the door and get more food. He is so voracious that he eats all the cheese and all the fish and he does not stop. His friend asks, "Do you need to sleep now?" The boy is voracious, so he says, "Yes, but I want to eat one more story of food first!" His friend gives him a red box of good food, and the boy is very happy. He sits at the table, and he is voracious again. He eats all of it and then he feels warm and good. The two friends look out the window at the sun and the green garden. The boy is voracious for food, but he is kind and funny here. He smiles and says, "We go to the park again, yes?" His friend smiles too, and they walk out the door into the warm morning.

The girl find a small fossil in the garden. It is an old bone from a bird that live very long ago. She hold the fossil close and feel happy. She tell her friend, and her friend want to see it too. The two girl sit at the door and open the fossil box to look at it more. They read a book and find two more old fossils in the room. One is red and one is blue, and they laugh. The boy come to the window and ask, "Do you know that old?" They say yes, and he help them dig in the garden for more. They find a big, dry rock with a funny little fish fossil in it. The cat run quick to the table and jump up to look too. The dog sit on the floor and eat some food, but he do not want to play with the old bone. The girl and her friend stand on the hill and look at the park. They think of all the new story they can make with the old fossils. The sun go down, the rain come, but they do not stop. They love to find and tell the story of the old.

The little hedgehog is very small and round, like a funny green ball.

He lives in a small house on a quiet street, and he is happy there.

Every morning, the hedgehog goes out to the garden to find food.

He looks for big fish or good cheese, but the garden is cold and wet.

The hedgehog is hungry, so he runs to the park to ask for help.

A kind dog there gives him some food, and the hedgehog is very happy.

He sits down on a chair and eats, and he feels warm and good.

One day, a bird comes to his window and says, "I know a new friend!"

The hedgehog is sad that the bird does not want to play with him, but then the bird calls out again, loud and funny.

The hedgehog laughs, and the bird laughs too, and they sit close and read a small book.

At night, the hedgehog goes back to his old house, and he smiles.

He thinks: a little hedgehog with a big heart is a very good friend.

The girl find an old key on the floor of her house. She look at it and she see that the key start to shimmer. It shine like the sun in the morning. She touch it and it shimmer more and more. The key seem to say, "Come with me." She run to her friend and tell her the story. Her friend laugh and say, "That key is very funny." But the girl do not stop. She open the door and the key shimmer big and bright. They walk into a room with a wall of water. The water go slow and cold, but the key shimmer again and again. Her friend ask, "Do you know where this is?" The girl smile and say, "I think the key help us find the garden." They walk out into the park and there is a big tree, a small house, and a happy blue bird. The key stop to shimmer when they get there. The girl sit down, her friend sit down, and they eat some food and read an old book. The sun come out and the rain stop. It is a very good day.

The road to the park is serpentine. It twists and turns and does not go straight at all.

A car drive on the serpentine road, and the driver has to turn left, then right, then left again.

The cat in the house look out the window and see the serpentine road, and she feel very hungry.

She run to the door, then stop, and think. The road is serpentine, but the food is not.

A dog find a big red box by the garden, and he want to open it. He is very hungry for food.

The cat call to the dog, but the dog do not hear her. They need to work together.

A small mouse hide close to the wall, and the serpentine path go right past him, and he smile.

The mouse tell his friend a funny story, and they laugh and laugh. The cold rain come down.

A bird sit on a tree and look at the serpentine road, and she say to her friend, "That is a long, slow road."

The two birds climb up to the top of the tree, and they see the serpentine road go into the green park.

A new friend come to the door, and the cat and the dog run to help him. They give him some cheese and a kind word.

The dog say, "We love to play on the serpentine road. It is so funny and good to run on!"

The cat smile, and she know that a serpentine path is not bad when a good friend is here

The cat sat on the old chair and looked out the window at the rain.

She was forlorn. So sad and all alone, like a little bird that lost its home.

The rain on the window made her look very blue and sad.

She did not run, she did not jump, she did not play. A forlorn cat on a cold day.

The dog in the garden could hear her and he wanted to help.

He walked to the door and looked in, but the cat did not come out.

The dog sit down on the floor and be quiet, for he too felt forlorn.

Then the girl at the house open the door and see them both.

She give the cat some food and the dog some water, and they all sit and wait.

The cat eat the food, but then she look at the dog and the dog look at the cat, and they feel sad again.

The girl ask, "Do you need a friend?"

The cat stand up, walk to the girl, and then she sit on her hand. A forlorn cat now happy here.

The dog and the cat and the girl all smile, and the old room feel warm and good.

The girl read a story to them, and the cat and the dog close their eyes and sleep.

No more rain, no more cold, no more lost. Just a kind new day.

The morning sun is out, but then the cold rain starts. The rain is not big or loud, but it is new and funny. The day is not good. A blizzard comes to the town. The blizzard is very big. It is so big that the snow is not slow or quiet. It is loud and fast. The snow is white and cold, and it is all the color of the blizzard. The blizzard is like a big, cold, white wall that comes down from the sky. The cat and the dog do not want to go out into the blizzard. They hide inside the house. The bird in the tree does not want to hide. It is hungry and it thinks it can catch some food in the blizzard. The bird is very slow, and the blizzard is very quick. The bird does not find the food. She is sad. The cat sees the sad bird and wants to help. She opens a window and the cold snow and rain come in with the story. The dog and the cat and the bird all sit close. They eat some cheese and read a book. The blizzard is not so bad now. The sun comes out again, and the whole town is warm and happy. The bird gives the cat and the dog a funny look. They smile and say, "We love you, friend."

The old house on the street has a big cellar. A small, dark room down under the floor.

No one knows when the cellar was made, and no one knows what is in it.

One day, a girl walks up to the door of the house and asks to see the cellar.

The old man at the door says, "Come in, come in, but do not go too close to the wall."

She finds a cold, quiet room with old floor and big stone walls.

She sees a funny old chair and a small table, but there is no door to the cellar.

Then she hears a loud, funny sound. A cat, a dog, or a bird? She is not sure.

She looks down and sees a small mouse run quick and quiet into the dark floor.

The mouse is very hungry, and the girl is very sad, for she has no food for him.

She gives him some old cheese from a box, and he stops and sits and starts to eat.

The girl and the mouse look at one another, and the mouse gives her one big, warm, green eye.

The girl smiles, and the old story of the cellar and the mouse is now a good, happy story.

The cat is hungry, and she needs to find some food.

She sees a big house with a blue door and a green garden.

A small bird sits in the tree, and the bird has a riddle. A funny question that is not easy to solve.

The riddle is: "I have keys but no hand. I have space but no room. What am I?"

The cat is not happy about this riddle, but she is kind, so she asks the bird to help her think.

The bird says, "Think of a door. Yes, a door!"

The cat laughs and says, "No, no, that is not it!"

Then the cat runs to the old house, and she finds a key on the floor.

She opens the box, and there is a small fish, and the fish is very cold.

The cat is very happy, and she gives the fish to her friend the dog.

The dog is hungry too, and now they eat and play in the park.

The bird in the tree smiles and says, "A good riddle is like a new friend. It makes the day warm and good."

The cat and the dog sit close to the tree, and they hear the rain on the wall.

They do not feel sad now. They feel warm, and they sleep with a big smile.

The old boat sits in the water, and a big cat is on it. The cat is an anchor. She does not run or jump, she just sits and holds the boat down. The boat is slow, but the cat is steady, and the boat does not move. A dog comes to the water and looks at the cat. The dog is hungry, but the cat is an anchor and she does not go. A man in a red hat calls out to the dog. He asks him to come, but the dog is too hungry to hear him. Then the cat stands up, and the boat starts to move. The dog sees this and runs to the water. The cat is still an anchor, and the boat is slow and quiet again. The dog is happy now. He finds food in the water and eats. The cat looks at him, and the dog smiles. They sit close, and the cat is the anchor for the day. The man in the red hat laughs and says, "I like that cat. She is very good at this." The dog and the cat feel warm and safe, and the sun is out. It is a good morning in the park.

The night was cold and the rain came down. A big fire started in the old house. A blaze of red and blue light up in the wall. The blaze was so big that it could be seen from the street. A cat sat at the window and looked out at the blaze. She did not run or hide, but she was very hungry. The cat walked to the door and then to the table, and she found some food. A dog lived in the garden, and he could hear the loud, happy fire in the house. He ran quick to the door, but the cat said, "No, friend, that is my work!" The dog sat down and the cat sat down too, and they looked at the blaze. The blaze was good and warm, and they did not feel sad. Soon the rain stopped and the sun came out. The cat and the dog looked at the new day. The cat said, "I love this fire. I want to see it again." The dog smiled and said, "Yes, one more story of warm light and good food."

The girl find a small pebble by the door.

The pebble is red and round, and she like it.

She put the pebble in her pocket and walk to the park.

At the park, she see a big dog and a small cat, and they do not like the pebble.

The dog run at the pebble, but he stop and look at it.

The cat sit on a chair and do not want the pebble.

The girl laugh and say, "No, no, this is for one friend!"

She give the pebble to her dog, and the dog is very happy.

The dog eat the pebble? No, he is kind, and he hold it close.

Then a bird come and sit on the wall, and the girl feel sad.

She ask her friend, "Do we need more water for the bird?"

Her friend find a box of food and give it to the bird.

The bird is happy, and the girl smile.

She and her friend read a book by the tree, and they feel warm in the sun.

The dog and the cat come and sit with them, and all of them feel good.

The girl think, "A small pebble, a kind dog, and a funny bird. This is a good day."

The river is very big and very cold. It runs through a gorge. A deep, narrow valley with two big walls. The gorge is so wide that the river looks small and funny there. A cat sits on a rock in the gorge and looks at the water. The cat is not sad. She is happy to be here. A dog runs into the gorge to find a lost key, but the key is not there. The dog is now very hungry, and he needs to eat. A bird flies up and calls out loud to the dog. The dog stops and thinks. He sees a small fish in the water of the gorge. The dog is quick, and he is good at this work. He jumps and runs fast to catch the fish. The cat in the gorge smiles and says, "You need more food, friend." The dog and the fish are warm and happy in the sun. They sit close to the old wall of the gorge and eat some good food. A boy reads a book at home and he is very kind. He gives the dog and the cat some food and a red hat. They all sleep well, and the gorge is quiet again.

There is an old barn on the farm. The barn is big and old and very quiet. A small cat live in the barn. She is hungry, so she look out of the window at the food in the garden. The cat go to the barn and find a box of old food there. She eat all the food and feel happy. One day, a dog run into the barn and see the cat. The dog is not happy, but the cat is kind. They sit close and do not say bad word to one another. The barn is a good place for them to sleep at night. A blue bird come to the barn too, and the cat and the dog help the bird get to the tree in the park. The bird is very funny when it jump up and down. The dog and the cat laugh, and the bird laugh too. The barn is not a bad home. It is a good home for all of them.

The rain stops and the sun comes out. A big meadow is here, green and warm. The meadow is full of flowers and a small bird. A girl walks into the meadow and sits down. She is sad, but the meadow feels good. She sees a red house far away, and she wants to go there. The red house is a beacon. It is bright and calls out to her in the morning sun. She runs to the beacon and opens the door. A kind old dog is in the room. He has a book and a chair and a funny hat. "Do you need help?" he says. "Yes!" she says, and they sit down to read. The meadow is not far, and the beacon is home now. The girl and the dog laugh, and the day is warm and happy again.

The old house on the street has a big cellar. A dark, cold room down below the floor.

No one knows when the cellar was built, but they say the house is old and the cellar is very old too.

At night, a small blue lantern can be seen close to the door of the cellar.

The lantern is not here during the day, so some think it is a funny, bad story.

A boy and his dog walk to the house one cold night and they find the blue lantern on the floor.

He opens the door of the cellar and the lantern is there too, on a old table in the dark room.

Inside the cellar, he sees a big chair, a box, and a funny green hat. But no one is here.

The boy and his dog look and look, and then the dog finds a key on the floor of the cellar.

He and his friend go up to the garden and call out, "We need help! The key is in the cellar!"

His friend runs quick to the door, and they open it and get the key. They laugh and feel happy.

The boy says, "I like this old house. The cellar and the lantern. They make it feel like a story."

His dog sits on the floor and they read an old book and eat some food there all morning.

The sea is big and the sky is blue. I want to go on a voyage. A long journey out to the sea. I get a small boat and I start my voyage. The water is cold and the boat is slow, but I do not stop. I see a harbor. A big safe place with a wall of green and blue. I stop my boat at the harbor and I eat some food there. A dog runs up to the harbor and he is very hungry. I ask him to sit and I give him some cheese. Then I hear a loud story from a old cat on a chair in the harbor house. The cat says his name is Friend and he loves the sea. We laugh and we feel happy again. I tell Friend that I need to make one more voyage, so he and the dog and I all get back into the boat. We look out at the big sea and we smile. A good voyage is not too long, and a good harbor is always here to help you.

The boy walks to the orchard. He stops and looks at all the big trees. He is very hungry, and he is ravenous. He wants to eat more food now, not later. He sees a red apple close to the wall of the orchard. He is so ravenous that he runs quick to get it. He sits down and eats the apple, and he feels happy. Then he looks up and sees a old bird in the tree. The bird has a funny blue hat on her head. The boy is ravenous again in the morning, and he needs more food. He finds a box of cheese at home, but his friend does not want to help him. He goes to the park and finds a fossil. It is an old, cold bone from long ago, and the boy does not know the story of this fossil. He sits by the window and reads a book about the fossil. He is ravenous one more day, and he asks his friend for some fish. They laugh and call him ravenous, and he smiles. He is happy to find new food and new stories to read. He does not feel sad now. He is kind and good, and he and the old bird in the orchard look out at the warm sun.
